scored_hypotheses.claim: The Voynich Manuscript encodes a late medieval Moravian (Old Czech/Slavic) dialect using a construct

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: Zipf exponent (0.8946) strongly indicates natural language structure, supporting linguistic encoding generally. However, statistics don't specifically identify Moravian over other languages, and the claim about Gujarati/Gojri plant names contradicts a Moravian-only hypothesis. Proper testing requires Moravian-specific glyph frequency comparison and multifolio decoding verification, not currently provided.

test_result.test: claude_evaluation

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
